focus: maintainability: system-spec-kit skill-doc alignment for drift-marker and generated-metadata pruning surfaces

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Two operating documents and one cross-skill integration surface define maintenance and destructive pruning behavior.

targetSelection.selectionReason: The iteration plan names the system-spec-kit skill, changed references, the scripts pruning contract, and deep-loop surfaces that cite their behavior.
omittedHighRiskTargets: No implementation mutation target was reviewed; the iteration was restricted to documentation-alignment.

searchLedger.invariant: The skill must direct script users to a current, bounded script architecture authority.

searchActions.result: SKILL.md names scripts/README.md as the script architecture authority and the README identifies current entrypoints and source boundaries.

searchLedger.rationale: The documents form an intentional authority chain without a contradictory ownership claim.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct-read comparison found no unsupported or conflicting contract.

searchLedger.invariant: Destructive generated-metadata pruning must preserve report-before-apply confirmation, scope binding, stale-report refusal, and recovery guidance.

searchActions.queryOrPath: scripts README generated metadata pruning section
searchActions.result: The contract requires --prune-report followed by --prune with the reviewed contentHash under identical scope controls, refuses stale reports, and specifies backup and restore behavior.

searchLedger.rationale: The operator contract exposes the full safety boundary rather than a shorthand destructive command.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: All required safeguards are explicitly documented.

searchLedger.invariant: The deep-review integration surface must cite the canonical Spec Kit resolver and ownership boundary rather than a retired local path.

searchActions.queryOrPath: system-spec-kit references in system-deep-loop deep-review surface
searchActions.result: The deep-review skill cites resolveArtifactRoot from the system-spec-kit shared module and names system-spec-kit as the command-owned state and continuity owner.

searchLedger.rationale: The cross-skill reference points to the current shared seam and preserves the ownership distinction.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No stale local resolver or conflicting owner claim was found.

reason: The skill-to-scripts README authority chain is explicit and non-contradictory.
Report, confirmation, stale-report refusal, and recovery guidance are all explicit.
The deep-review skill names the canonical shared resolver and Spec Kit ownership boundary.

detail: skill_agent and documentation seam checks passed; checklist and non-document overlays are not applicable.
